decision.reason: The finite public bit-RAM has a fixed public opcode set, explicit public C typing, decision preservation, and a polynomial standard simulator. The reverse frontend from arbitrary standard machines is still not supplied.

next_highest_yield_item.action: Define a uniform compiler from standard deterministic Turing/RAM machine executions into finite_public_bit_ram programs and prove polynomial overhead.

non_claims: This does not provide a frontend from arbitrary standard Turing machines to finite_public_bit_ram.
This does not prove P_CVX equals standard P.
This does not classify uncompiled native machine instructions as C.
This does not allow X extractor/oracle/advice operations inside public P.

compiled_events_are_public_c.needed: The source machine simulation must not introduce V or X events.

evidence.applies_to: executions already represented in the finite_public_bit_ram model
evidence.decision_preservation: the compiled trace preserves the source machine emit_decision and halt instruction outputs
evidence.local_event_verification: one opcode/class-code lookup plus public payload checks

decision_preservation_inherited.needed: The compiled execution must preserve emit_decision and halt outputs.

evidence.bit_and: read two public bits, write their conjunction
evidence.bit_not: read one public bit, write its complement
evidence.bit_or: read two public bits, write their disjunction
evidence.bit_xor: read two public bits, write their public xor
evidence.branch: read a public condition bit and update the program counter
evidence.compare_zero: scan a public word and emit whether all bits are zero
evidence.copy: copy a public word between public registers or memory cells
evidence.emit_decision: copy the public decision register to the output channel
evidence.input_read: copy a public input bit or word into public memory
evidence.loop_tick: increment a public step counter and continue
evidence.ram_load: read a public-addressed public memory cell
evidence.ram_store: write a public-addressed public memory cell

every_opcode_has_standard_simulation_cas.needed: Each finite public bit-RAM opcode must have an explicit standard simulator case.

opcode_set_matches_compiler_rules.needed: Every source opcode admitted by the schema must have a compiler/class-code rule.

polynomial_overhead_bound_declared.needed: The standard simulator overhead must be polynomial in source runtime and public width.

program_schema_fixes_finite_public_bit_r.needed: The source machine must be a fixed finite public bit-RAM model.

theorem.bound: For runtime T, public word width W, and program size P, a RAM simulation costs O(T*(W+log P)); a multitape Turing simulation costs O(T*(W+log P)^2).
theorem.name: Finite Public Bit-RAM Standard Simulation
theorem.statement: Every finite_public_bit_ram execution admitted by the repository program schema and compiled by the universal trace compiler is simulated by a standard deterministic polynomial-time RAM/Turing machine with polynomial overhead, while preserving public decisions and using only public C operations.
